case.title: UNITED STATES v. DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE OF ILLINOIS

opinion.h1: UNITED STATES v. DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE OF ILLINOIS, 371 U.S. 21 (1962)
opinion.h4: UNITED STATES ET AL. v. DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE OF ILLINOIS ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF ILLINOIS. No. 235. Decided October 15, 1962.
opinion.p: Solicitor General Cox, Assistant Attorney General Oberdorfer, I. Henry Kutz and William Massar for the United States.
John H. Caruthers for Olin Mathieson Chemical Corp., appellant.
opinion.h4: SOUTHEASTERN AVIATION, INC., v. HURD, ADMINISTRATOR, 371 U.S. 21 (1962)
opinion.p: 371 U.S. 21 SOUTHEASTERN AVIATION, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS SOUTHEAST AIRLINES, v. HURD. ADMINISTRATOR. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TENNESSEE, EASTERN DIVISION. No. 286. Decided October 15, 1962.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Russell R. Kramer and Erma Griffith Greenwood for appellant.
Howard E. Wilson and Preston H. Taylor for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
